chunks.title: Zophar's Rebuke
chunks.reason: Zophar opens his speech by challenging Job's verbosity and claiming God will reveal the secrets of wisdom.

chunks.title: The Depth of God
chunks.reason: Zophar argues for the incomprehensible nature of God's wisdom and contrasts it with human foolishness.

chunks.title: Call to Repentance
chunks.reason: Zophar exhorts Job to repent and promises restoration if he turns from wickedness, contrasting it with the fate of the wicked.
